message:
feat: DeferredArcCache, ext4 xattr block allocation, edge-case tests, std::sync::Mutex fixes

- Add DeferredArcCache<D> wrapper combining write-back ArcCache + FlushDaemon
  with correct drop ordering (daemon first, cache second) and shutdown()
- Fix ext4 write path: allocate external xattr block on NoSpace instead of
  propagating error when inline region exhausted and no block exists yet
- Fix std::sync::Mutex .lock() calls missing .expect() on runtime.scheduler
  in ffs-block and ffs-mvcc test/lib code (21 occurrences)
- Add edge-case tests: symlink loop detection (ext4), timestamp nsec
  roundtrip (inode), punch_hole extent splitting (extent)
- Fix minor issues in ffs-repair evidence/scrub and ffs-mvcc persist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1cc","title":"FUSE: Define MVCC snapshot/transaction policy for FUSE operations","description":"Goal: decide how MVCC integrates with the kernel request model.

Questions:
- Does each FUSE op run at a single snapshot (read-only), or do we start a transaction per open()/release()?
- How do we handle readdir+lookup consistency?

Deliverables:
- A documented policy in COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC.
- Implementation hooks in ffs-fuse to acquire snapshot/tx per request.

Acceptance:
- Concurrency tests show consistent reads under concurrent writers (once writes exist).
- Policy is simple enough to reason about and test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:39.467984753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:50:37.112290445Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:50:37.112268945Z","close_reason":"done: documented Phase 7A policy + implemented FsOps request-scope hooks in ffs-fuse with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cc","depends_on_id":"bd-2t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:52.176803421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cq","title":"Track: btrfs On-Disk Parsing + Mapping (ffs-ondisk)","description":"Implement btrfs superblock parsing, sys_chunk_array bootstrap mapping, single-device logical->physical mapping, and basic B-tree node/item parsing for read-only discovery.\n\nAcceptance: can open a btrfs image, validate superblock, map a logical address via sys_chunk, and iterate root tree items without panics; all guarded by fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:41.112572499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:27:28.404663163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:27:28.404641172Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-1fo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.414637386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.259271763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-kdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.337640505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-o76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.495838876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-z7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:16.963240254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-1cq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"btrfs bootstrap is different from ext4: you must parse sys_chunk_array to map logical->physical before you can even read the trees.

Early wins:
- parse superblock + sys_chunk
- map root/chunk_root
- parse nodes and walk root tree (read-only)

Multi-device and RAID profiles remain explicitly excluded until read-only parity is solid.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ds","title":"ffs-types: Finalize InodeNumber strategy (ext4 u32 vs btrfs u64)","description":"Context: ext4 stores inode numbers as u32; btrfs uses u64 objectids. The code currently standardizes on InodeNumber(u64) in ffs-types.

Goal: make the representation choice explicit and safe:
- keep InodeNumber(u64) canonical, but add per-format wrappers (Ext4InodeNumber(u32), BtrfsObjectId(u64)) OR
- switch to an enum/typed wrapper that prevents mixing domains.

Deliverables:
- Chosen representation documented in ffs-types docs + COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC.
- Conversion functions at parsing boundaries.
- Tests proving no silent truncation or sign/width bugs.

Acceptance:
- No crate invents its own inode/objectid types.
- Public APIs do not require callers to guess whether a u64 is ext4 or btrfs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:31.298330901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:54.151204622Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:54.151178934Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

 {"id":"bd-1mdk","title":"EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)","description":"# EPIC: Bw-Trees for Extent Indexes (Graveyard Entry 8.7)

## PURPOSE
Replace traditional B-tree extent indexes with Bw-Trees (Buzzword Trees) — lock-free delta-chain trees that allow concurrent readers and writers without any reader blocking. This is a long-term performance optimization for the extent index hot path.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7:
- Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): latch-free B-tree with delta chains
- Key insight: instead of in-place mutation, append delta records to page
- Readers never block: they traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest
- Writers use CAS to append deltas (no locks)
- Periodic consolidation merges delta chains back into base pages

## DESIGN

### Architecture
- Mapping table: PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>
- Delta chain: linked list of delta records (insert, delete, split, merge)
- Base page: consolidated B-tree node
- Consolidation: background thread merges delta chains when they exceed threshold

### Delta Records
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { split_key: ExtentKey, new_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_page_id: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```

### Concurrency
- Lookup: traverse mapping table -> delta chain -> base page (all read-only, no locks)
- Insert/Delete: create delta, CAS into mapping table (retry on conflict)
- Split/Merge: structural modification via delta + CAS
- Consolidation: create new base page, CAS to replace delta chain

### Extent Index Integration
- Replace extent B-tree in ffs-ext4 and ffs-btrfs with Bw-Tree
- Same lookup API: find_extent(inode, logical_block) -> PhysicalExtent
- Same mutation API: insert_extent, delete_extent, split_extent

## CURRENT PRIORITY
P2 — performance optimization, not blocking correctness. Current B-tree works correctly.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Lock-free lookup: readers never block on writers
- [ ] CAS-based insert/delete: no mutex on hot path
- [ ] Delta chain consolidation prevents unbounded chain growth
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup throughput under concurrent writers vs B-tree with locks
- [ ] Correctness: all B-tree invariants maintained (sorted, balanced, split/merge correct)
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for current extent index

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E benchmark (bd-1mdk.4) shows improvement over locked B-tree
3. Unit tests (bd-1mdk.3) pass
4. Delta chain consolidation compacts correctly
5. Mapping table is lock-free (no mutex in hot path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:46.207227957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541669980Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:20:07.541602964Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","index","lockfree"],"comments":[{"id":98,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mdk.1","title":"Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core","description":"# Implement Bw-Tree mapping table and delta chain core

## GOAL
Implement the foundational data structures for a Bw-Tree (Levandoski et al., ICDE 2013): the mapping table (PageId -> AtomicPtr<PageDelta>) and delta chain (linked list of insert/delete/split/merge deltas appended via CAS).

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7: Bw-Trees are latch-free B-trees that replace in-place mutation with append-only delta records. Readers traverse the delta chain from newest to oldest, never blocking. Writers use compare-and-swap (CAS) to atomically append deltas. This eliminates all reader-side synchronization.

## DESIGN

### Mapping Table
```rust
struct MappingTable {
    pages: Vec<AtomicPtr<PageDelta>>,
    next_page_id: AtomicU64,
}
```
- Fixed-size array of atomic pointers (one per page/node)
- All B-tree operations go through mapping table indirection
- Enables lock-free structural modifications (split/merge update a pointer, not a tree)

### Delta Chain
```rust
enum PageDelta {
    Insert { key: ExtentKey, value: ExtentValue, next: *const PageDelta },
    Delete { key: ExtentKey, next: *const PageDelta },
    Split { separator: ExtentKey, new_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Merge { removed_sibling: PageId, next: *const PageDelta },
    Base(BTreeNode),
}
```
- Each delta is a small record prepended to the chain via CAS
- Lookup: walk chain applying deltas to reconstruct current state
- Base page: consolidated snapshot of all deltas (periodic compaction target)

### CAS Append Protocol
1. Read current head pointer from mapping table
2. Create new delta with next = current head
3. CAS mapping_table[page_id] from current to new delta
4. On CAS failure: retry (another writer appended concurrently)

### Memory Management
- Delta nodes allocated via crossbeam-epoch (same EBR as MVCC versions)
- Old deltas freed after consolidation and epoch advance
- No manual memory management or unsafe deallocation

## RUST IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Use `crossbeam::epoch::{Atomic, Owned, Shared, Guard}` for all pointers
- `unsafe` blocks required for Shared pointer dereference (justified by epoch pin safety)
- #[repr(C)] for delta variants to ensure stable layout
- Consider cache-line alignment for mapping table entries to avoid false sharing

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] MappingTable supports allocate_page, get_page, cas_page operations
- [ ] Delta chain supports Insert, Delete append via CAS
- [ ] Lookup correctly traverses delta chain to find current value
- [ ] CAS retry loop handles concurrent append conflicts
- [ ] crossbeam-epoch used for all delta allocation and reclamation
- [ ] Unit tests: single-threaded insert/delete/lookup round-trip
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent CAS append (2+ threads, verify no lost updates)
- [ ] Unit tests: delta chain traversal returns correct merged state
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for CAS attempts, retries, and chain length at lookup time","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704819462Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:19.704794174Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bwtree","core","lockfree"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:01.435985132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":81,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"},{"id":165,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: general
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.8 Runtime Decision Core + 0.19 Evidence Ledger
EV score: 9 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: B
Budgeted mode: controller_tick_ms=1000, policy_timeout_ms=200
Fallback trigger: static config profile

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8tr","title":"ext4: Implement inode location math helper (inode -> table block + offset)","description":"Goal: given (inode_number, superblock fields, group desc), compute where the inode bytes live on disk.

Deliverables:
- Pure helper: inode_number -> (group, index_in_group).
- Use group desc inode_table pointer and inode_size to compute:
  - table_start_block
  - inode_byte_offset_from_table_start
  - absolute byte offset on device
- Handle ext4 inode numbering rules (inode numbers start at 1; 0 invalid).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for boundary inodes: 1, inodes_per_group, inodes_per_group+1, last inode.
- Overflow/invalid inputs produce ParseError/FfsError (no panics).

 {"id":"bd-cebm","title":"Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees","description":"# Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees

## GOAL
Implement correct fsync and flush semantics for FUSE mount, ensuring durability guarantees match user expectations.

## BACKGROUND
fsync is the primary durability contract:
- `fsync(fd)` must ensure all data written to fd is on stable storage
- `fdatasync(fd)` same but may skip non-essential metadata
- `flush` is called on close (cleanup, not durability)

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. fsync Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsync(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // 1. Get inode from fh
        // 2. Commit any pending MVCC transaction for this file
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries for this inode's blocks
        // 4. If !datasync: also flush inode metadata block
        // 5. Wait for I/O completion
        // 6. If repair_eager: refresh RaptorQ symbols for dirty groups
        // 7. reply.ok() or reply.error(errno)
    }
}
```

### 2. Flush Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn flush(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, lock_owner: u64, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Called on close - release resources, no durability guarantee
        // 1. Release file handle
        // 2. Decrement open count
        // 3. If last close + unlinked: queue for deletion
        // 4. reply.ok()
    }
}
```

### 3. fsyncdir Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsyncdir(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Same as fsync but for directories
        // Ensures directory entries are durable
    }
}
```

### 4. Integration Points
- **MVCC**: fsync triggers transaction commit
- **ARC Cache**: fsync flushes dirty entries for inode
- **RaptorQ**: fsync may trigger eager symbol refresh
- **Journal**: fsync ensures journal commit block written

### 5. Error Handling
- I/O error during fsync -> EROFS or EIO
- MVCC conflict -> EAGAIN (retry)
- Timeout -> EIO with detailed logging

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: fsync flushes dirty blocks for inode
2. Unit: fdatasync skips metadata
3. Integration: fsync + crash + recovery -> data durable
4. Integration: fsync on RO mount -> EROFS
5. E2E: Write + fsync + unmount + remount -> data present

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- fsync start (debug): inode, datasync flag
- fsync cache flush (trace): block count, dirty bytes
- fsync commit (trace): commit_seq
- fsync complete (debug): duration_us, blocks_flushed
- fsync error (warn): inode, error, context

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. fsync guarantees data is on stable storage
2. fsync failure returns correct errno
3. No data loss if process crashes after successful fsync
4. Performance: fsync of single small file < 100ms","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:10.359139338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214188659Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214168702Z","close_reason":"Verified fsync/flush/fsyncdir durability semantics and focused rch test evidence; criteria satisfied","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","durability","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:18.909563807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.663794504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Starting implementation now. Planned touch set: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs, crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs tests as needed. Please avoid overlapping edits.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:39Z"},{"id":210,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (Codex): implemented FUSE flush/fsync/fsyncdir path with durability plumbing. Added RequestOp variants, FsOps trait methods (default semantics), OpenFs ext4 durability sync via device sync, and ffs-fuse handlers for flush/fsync/fsyncdir with proper RO behavior and errno mapping. Added/updated unit tests in ffs-core and validated ffs-fuse tests. Full required gate chain passed: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:44:11Z"},{"id":212,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (Codex): added durability proof/optimization slice.  {"id":"bd-huh","title":"EPIC: ext4 Write Path","description":"# EPIC: ext4 Write Path

## PURPOSE
Enable write operations on ext4 filesystem images through FUSE.

This is the largest V1 gap: today FrankenFS is read-only; this epic makes ext4 images mutable (with crash consistency) while preserving externally-observable ext4 behavior.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gaps:
- full compatibility-mode write-path equivalence

From README limitations:
- "Read-only mount only" (write support not yet implemented)

The ext4 write path requires orchestrating:
1. Transaction boundaries (MVCC for concurrency control)
2. Block allocation and freeing (ext4 mballoc semantics)
3. Extent tree mutation (insert/split/merge + overlap handling)
4. Inode updates and checksums
5. Directory entry mutation (create/unlink/rename)
6. Journal write path (JBD2-compatible logging) and replay integration
7. FUSE + CLI surfaces (RW mount flag and write ops)

## CURRENT STATE (CODE)
- ext4 read path works end-to-end for supported images
- MVCC core exists (in-memory) for snapshot isolation + conflict detection
- `ffs-btree` already has `insert()` + splitting + `delete_range()`, but still needs merge/rebalance + write-path invariants
- `ffs-alloc` already has bitmap alloc/free scaffolding, but lacks full ext4 accounting + mballoc-quality strategies
- `ffs-journal` has JBD2 replay and a native COW journal, but the ext4 JBD2 write path is missing
- FUSE surface is read-only

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Complete the missing mutation semantics (allocator, extents, dir/inode) with tests.
2. Implement journal writer + integrate commit/checkpoint semantics.
3. Expose write ops via `FsOps` and FUSE with an explicit `--rw` flag.
4. Add E2E write suite that proves persistence and basic crash behavior.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `echo "test" > /mnt/ffs/newfile.txt` works.
2. `mkdir /mnt/ffs/newdir` works.
3. `rm /mnt/ffs/file` works.
4. Changes persist after unmount/remount.
5. Crash recovery via journal replay.
6. Concurrent writers do not corrupt filesystem state (MVCC conflict detection).

## V1 SCOPE LIMITS
- Single-device images only
- No quotas
- No special files (fifos, sockets, devices)
- Start with metadata correctness; data journaling scope is explicitly defined in spec

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §5 (Write Path)
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §2 (ext4 write behavior)

## Success Criteria
1. `scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh` passes on a generated ext4 image and produces actionable logs/artifacts.
2. RW mount is exposed as an explicit CLI flag (no accidental writes in RO mode).
3. Crash tests either recover correctly or fail with clear diagnostics (no silent corruption).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:15.648865297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373119558Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373100072Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":116,"issue_id":"bd-huh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: format_path

EV score: 10.666666666666666 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: txn_retry_budget=3, read_latency_p99_budget_ms=50
Fallback trigger: read-only mode + strict compatibility checks

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
